# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

Prevent postgres weak auth.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# pg_hba.conf file configuration example to allow local connections from application servers and set up host-based authentication rules (user roles) 
local   all             app           peer     # Allow access for 'app' user role on the database server via a client in the same network. This is typically not recommended unless you have special needs, as it could open your PostgreSQL instance to security risks if misused or exposed directly over public networks (i.e., internal).
local   all             app           peer     # Allow access for 'app' user role on database server via a client in the same network segment and also from other hosts within this local network only, using TCP port 5432 by default but can be configured to use any available port (i.e., not recommended).
local   all             app           md5     # Allow access for 'app' user role on database server via a client in the same machine and also from other hosts within this local network only, using TCP/IP authentication mechanism by default but can be configured to use any available method (i.e., not recommended unless you have specific needs).
local   all             app           password    # Allow access for 'app' user role on database server via a client in the same machine and also from other hosts within this local network only, using TCP/IP authentication mechanism by default but can be configured to use any available method (i.e., not recommended unless you have specific needs).
